Fix histogram metrics with programmatic MeterFilter instead of YAML property

for http.server.requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix tooltip overflow detection for line-clamped cells

Previous scrollHeight check didn't work with -webkit-line-clamp.
Now temporarily unsets the clamp to measure true content height,
then compares against clamped height to decide if tooltip is needed.

Fix tooltip: no [object Object] on ports, only show when clipped

Tooltip now uses raw scalar value (skips arrays/objects like ports),
and only appears on mouseenter when the .cell div content overflows
its line-clamp. Non-clipped cells show no tooltip.

commit.message:
Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

Pivot edges now prefer host→host (not access→host) so BFS layer
assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix graph: WEB01 as pivot dest, table cell wrapping + tooltips

Graph fixes:
- pivotDestHosts built from actual edge rendering (replaces broken
  pivotDests built from raw destination text that never matched hosts)
- Fuzzy pivot source/destination matching: descriptive text like
  "DC01.pirate.htb (10.129.244.95) - gMSA..." now matches host IPs
  and subnet prefixes (192.168.100.0/24 matches host 192.168.100.2)
- WEB01 now correctly positioned as pivot destination from DC01 access
- Pivot edge labels truncated to first segment (≤40 chars)

Table improvements:
- Cells wrap up to 3 lines with CSS line-clamp instead of single-line
  truncation — shows much more context at a glance
- title attribute on every <td> shows full text on mouseover
- max-width increased from 300px to 400px

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add v0.51.0: Curve2D.parameterAtLength, interior tangent interpolation, Wire.fromCurve2D

Resolves GitHub issues #37, #38, and #39 to complete the 2D curve API:

- #37: Curve2D.parameterAtLength(_:from:) — GCPnts_AbscissaPoint exposes arc-length
  parameter lookup on any 2D curve, enabling precise curve trimming and placement
- #38: Curve2D.interpolate(through:tangents:closed:tolerance:) — interior tangent
  constraint overload using Geom2dAPI_Interpolate.Load(NCollection_Array1<gp_Vec2d>,
  Handle(NCollection_HArray1<bool>)) for per-point tangent control
- #39: Wire.fromCurve2D(_:origin:normal:xAxis:) — lifts a 2D parametric curve
  onto a 3D geometric plane via BRepBuilderAPI_MakeEdge(Geom2d_Curve, Geom_Surface)
  with BRepLib::BuildCurves3d to materialise the 3D curve representation

690 wrapped operations, 1062 tests across 290 suites — all passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: corregir bug critico de addons_recurrentes en flujo de pagos

Dos problemas corregidos:

1. generar_link_pago() ya no guarda addons_recurrentes antes del pago.
   Solo se guarda cuando Wompi confirma la transaccion (webhook).
   Antes: si contabilidad nunca pagaba, los add-ons quedaban registrados
   para la proxima renovacion aunque no se hubieran cobrado.

2. El webhook ahora ACUMULA en addons_recurrentes en lugar de reemplazar.
   Antes: si el cliente tenia 2 tecnicos y compraba 1 mas, quedaba
   registrado solo 1 (el ultimo). En la renovacion se cobraba solo 1.
   Ahora: se suman correctamente → 2 + 1 = 3 tecnicos en renovacion.

Aplica a los 3 flujos: pago directo, link contabilidad ADDON, link plan+addon.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: tiempo transcurrido y restante en boletos pendientes

- Columna Fecha muestra "hace X h" + "quedan Y h" para boletos pendientes
- Código de color: gris → amarillo (50% del límite) → naranja (<2h) → rojo (expirado)
- Actualización en vivo cada minuto sin recargar la página
- Tiempo límite tomado de la configuración de cancelación del admin

commit.message:
feat(lsp): support rename symbol for fragments, operations, and variables (#717)

## Summary

- Implement `textDocument/rename` and `textDocument/prepareRename` LSP
methods
- Fragment rename is project-wide across all spreads and the definition
- Operation rename and variable rename are file-local

## Changes

- Add `RenameResult` POD type in `graphql-ide`
- Add `rename.rs` module with `prepare_rename()` and `rename()`
functions
- Wire up LSP handlers and register `rename_provider` capability with
`prepare_provider: true`
- Add 7 tests covering fragments (project-wide + from spread),
operations, variables, and rejection of non-renameable symbols

Closes #296

## Test Plan

1. Open a `.graphql` file with fragment definitions and spreads
2. Place cursor on a fragment spread name and trigger rename (F2 in VS
Code)
3. Verify the prepare rename highlights the fragment name
4. Enter a new name and confirm all spreads and the definition are
updated across files
5. Repeat for operation names (file-local only) and variable references
($var in arguments)
6. Verify that renaming a type name or field name is rejected (cursor on
a type/field returns no rename range)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
fix: Vercel 배포 빌드 에러 수정

- (dashboard)/page.tsx에 dynamic export 추가하여 client-reference-manifest 에러 해결
- 루트 page.tsx 리디렉트를 /ai-chat으로 통일

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/explore curtis brag codebase fb3 gz (#155)

* Add authenticated cluster control panel dashboard

Create /cluster/dashboard page with password-protected controls for
managing the phone cluster: quick actions (wake/sleep/start/stop/restart/
reboot/update), mining controls with level slider, display mode switching,
brightness control, SSH command execution with output polling, URL browsing,
screenshot capture/viewing, and command queue/history monitoring. Uses
existing cluster-control.js API with sessionStorage auth. Move cluster.astro
to cluster/index.astro for proper subdirectory routing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix security: add auth to flush-queue and auth-check endpoint

- Add password auth to flush-queue GET endpoint (was unauthenticated)
- Add dedicated auth-check endpoint so login doesn't fire a real debug
  command to node1
- Update dashboard to use auth-check for login and pass password to
  flush-queue

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove old static dashboard.html that was overriding Astro page

public/cluster/dashboard.html (6090-line standalone page) was being
served by Netlify instead of the Astro-built dashboard at
src/pages/cluster/dashboard.astro. The static file took priority,
so the new control panel with auth was never being served.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove stale redirects pointing /cluster/dashboard to deleted HTML file

netlify.toml had explicit redirects from /cluster/dashboard to
/cluster/dashboard.html which no longer exists (deleted in previous
commit). These redirects prevented Astro's dashboard/index.html from
being served. Removing them lets Netlify serve the Astro-built page.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix poll script: add PC nodes and proper target group expansion

- Add PC nodes (neo, vikixii, nexus-prime, steamdeck) to cluster-nodes.conf
  with "pc" role, using Tailscale hostnames for SSH resolution
- Add PC_NODES group and resolve_target_nodes() helper for consistent
  target expansion across all commands
- "all" now targets phones+PCs for general commands (start/stop/restart/
  mining/reboot/ssh), phones only for phone-specific commands
- "phones" targets phone nodes only, "pcs" targets PC nodes only
- Phone-specific commands (wake/sleep/display-mode/browse/brightness/
  screenshot) return "skipped" when targeted at "pcs"
- control-plane nodes now correctly included in PHONE_NODES

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining commands: scope to phones only and capture error output

- mining-start/stop/level now use "phones" scope instead of "all" —
  PCs don't have xmrig so targeting them caused false failures
- mining-start now captures per-node stdout and reports it back via
  the output field, so the dashboard shows WHY nodes failed (e.g.
  "xmrig not installed", service status, etc.)
- Fixed mining-start command to print errors to stdout instead of
  stderr so run_on_node captures them properly

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig systemd service if missing

The phones run systemd but had no xmrig.service file, causing
mining-start to fail with "Unit xmrig.service could not be found".
Now mining-start auto-creates the service file, reloads systemd,
and ensures the xmrig binary symlink exists before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig config.json if missing

xmrig was exiting with status=2 because /etc/xmrig/config.json
didn't exist on the phones. Now mining-start auto-creates both
the config (with pool/wallet/worker settings) and the systemd
service file per-node before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: use printf instead of nested heredocs

The previous approach used nested heredocs in a function inside a
case block, which crashed BusyBox ash on the phones after update.
Simplified to a single-quoted variable with printf for both config
and service file creation. Uses hostname for worker name.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix bad merge: remove stale function code from mining-start

GitHub merge produced a frankenstein with old function-based code
(MSCRIPT_BOT, build_mining_start_cmd) mixed into the printf version.
This caused a syntax error on line 487 crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Move MINING_START_CMD to top of script for BusyBox ash compat

BusyBox ash on the phones fails to parse multi-line single-quoted
strings containing if/else/fi inside case blocks. Moving the command
to the top level as a single-line string fixes the parse error at
line 487 that was crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

(MSCRIPT_BOT, build_mining_start_cmd) mixed into the printf version.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Move MINING_START_CMD to top of script for BusyBox ash compat

BusyBox ash on the phones fails to parse multi-line single-quoted
strings containing if/else/fi inside case blocks. Moving the command
to the top level as a single-line string fixes the parse error at
line 487 that was crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove duplicate MINING_START_CMD from case block

The inline multi-line MINING_START_CMD inside the case block causes
BusyBox ash to fail parsing. Only the top-level single-line definition
(line 60) should exist.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Add mining-status diagnostic command

Adds a mining-status command that checks xmrig process, systemd service
status, HTTP API reachability, config existence, and recent logs on all
phone nodes. Helps diagnose why mining shows offline on dashboard.

- New mining-status handler in poll-cluster-commands.sh
- Added to valid commands in cluster-control.js
- Added Status button next to Start/Stop in dashboard mining controls

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Show command output in dashboard history

History entries with output (mining-status, ssh, etc.) are now clickable
to expand/collapse a pre-formatted output panel. Also added error/partial
result icons and increased history container height.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Poll and display mining-status output in dashboard

mining-status and mining-start now poll for completion and show output
in the SSH output area, so diagnostic results are immediately visible
without needing to expand history entries. Increased poll timeout to 60s
for multi-node commands.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Make command output persistent with close button

Output panel now stays visible until manually closed with X button.
Scrolls into view when results arrive. Prevents output from appearing
to disappear when page auto-refreshes history.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix duplicate sshOutput div and textContent from rebase

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

---------

commit.message:
Feat: Portal PIN — salted SHA-256 hashing + 6-digit + CSV auto-generate

- generatePortalPin upgraded from 4 to 6 digits (900k possibilities)
- AddResidentModal: hash PIN with hashPin(pin, residentId) before storing;
  plaintext PIN passed to WhatsApp helper only (never stored)
- EditResidentModal: show-once PIN reset — plaintext shown in green chip,
  hash written on save; current PIN display hidden (non-readable)
- handleImport (CSV/Excel): generates unique 6-digit PIN per resident,
  hashes all PINs in parallel (Promise.all); step 3 shows one-time PIN
  table (unit → name → PIN) with copy-all button
- validateResidentAccess: now async; live Supabase residents use hashPin
  comparison with graceful migration fallback (regex detects 64-char hex
  vs legacy plaintext — both work during transition)
- LoginPage: await validateResidentAccess; error message updated to 6 digits
